strengths: Complete veto-holder coverage: CIO, CISO, Einkauf, and technical evaluator all present with distinct decision roles
Betriebsrat correctly identified as consultation requirement for managed service contracts affecting headcount
Pain points include concrete regulatory deadlines (NIS2 October 2026, SAP ECC EOL 2027) and specific thresholds (SektVO EUR 431K)
Buying criteria read like actual RFP requirements — BSI-C5 Type 2, Rahmenvertrag, SektVO compliance, EVB-IT templates
Every pain point maps to at least one proposition: SAP migration → cloud-migration, sovereignty → sovereign-cloud, multi-cloud → managed-hyperscaler, OT/IT convergence → hybrid-cloud
Four distinct profiles with non-overlapping pain points enable genuine multi-threaded account engagement
Named customers include specific tech stack data (RWE: RISE with SAP on Azure, E.ON: existing T-Systems reference) enabling immediate account planning
Six specific deal stall points with timelines allow AE to build realistic close plans
concerns: Named customer fit scores are all 'high' — some differentiation in fit levels would help AEs prioritize account sequencing
recommendations: OPTIONAL: Consider varying fit scores across named customers to signal priority order for account pursuit

strengths: Native energy sector terminology throughout: KRITIS, NIS2, BSI-C5, IS-U, Messstellenbetrieb, Netzleitstelle, SektVO, SCADA, Rahmenvertrag
All information sources verified as real and currently active: E-world energy & water, Handelsblatt Energietagung, BDEW Kongress, it-sa, BSI IT-Grundschutz-Kompendium, BME
Temporal references are current: NIS2 October 2026 deadline, SAP ECC EOL 2027, correct SektVO thresholds
Regulatory framework correctly maps to German energy utility context: SektVO (not VOL/A), BSIG §8a, BSI-C5 Type 2

optional_improvements.description: Differentiate named customer fit scores (not all 'high') to help sales team prioritize account pursuit order

synthesis.revision_guidance: Profile is ready for downstream use. All three perspectives score 85+. The only optional improvement is differentiating named customer fit scores for account prioritization, but this does not affect profile quality. No revision required.
